prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "自行车卷腹" (bicycle crunch on the floor) exercise, side view from low angle on the right side of the body, body lying on the floor. Person posture: Athletic person LYING ON THEIR BACK ON A YOGA MAT ON THE FLOOR (SUPINE position) — the LOWER BACK and HIPS remain pressed onto the mat. Only the upper torso (head and shoulders) is slightly lifted off the mat by the abdominal contraction. Both hands lightly behind the head with elbows out. The RIGHT KNEE is pulled HIGH toward the chest (right hip and knee deeply flexed). The LEFT LEG is FULLY EXTENDED forward parallel to and just above the floor (left foot hovering near floor level). The torso is rotated so the LEFT ELBOW reaches diagonally toward the RIGHT KNEE — the left elbow and right knee meet in the middle of the body. Plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS: this person is LYING DOWN, NOT seated, NOT sitting up at 45 degrees, NOT in any kind of seated V-position. The body is HORIZONTAL on the floor with only a small abdominal crunch lifting the head/shoulders. Show the floor/mat clearly underneath.. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: obliques (especially the side rotating across) and rectus abdominis. Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: hip flexors of the drawn-up knee and lower abs. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "腹斜肌" pointing to the obliques; "腹直肌" pointing to the rectus abdominis; "髂腰肌" pointing to the hip flexors. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
